We recommended that the Municipal mayor

Institute appropriate action to preserve the structure and equipment of the iceplant by seeking technical assistance from proper agency of consider possible divestment scheme to recover residual value of the project investment

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require the (a) end-users or implementing units, in conjunction with the BAC to (i) refrain from referring to a particular product brand in its Purchase Request and use instead the generic descriptions of the goods to be procured; and (ii) provide a written justification on how they determined the ABC of the procured antigen test kits and submit valid documents to support their computation; (b) BAC to (i) ensure that the ABCs of items to be procured are based on careful determination of price reasonableness; and (ii)  update the prices of items in the ABC before the conduct of actual bidding/procurement. We also recommended that Management procure antigen test kits that are approved by FDA to assure the quality and safety on the use of those tests.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) create an Inventory Committee who shall (i) in coordination with the Property Division/Unit, plan/strategize on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period  by preparing a Physical Inventory Plan; (ii) be temporarily relieved of  all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed; and (iii) ensure full implementation of “One Time Cleansing of PPE accounts”  in accordance with the guidelines of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; (b) enjoin the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary accounting entries to write-off/drop from the books of accounts the amounts lumped for the unreconciled balances due to conversion of accounts from OGAS to NGAS in accordance with Section 6.3.2 (e) of  COA Circular No. 2020-006.
We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a) General Services Officer to initiate the process of disposing the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445; and (b) Municipal Accountant to record the eventual disposal of properties.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) contractor to repair the defective and damaged portion of the identified roads and bridges pursuant to Section 62 of the Revised  IRR of RA 9184; and (b) Municipal Engineer to (i) include in its preparation of the Program of Works and Approved Budget for the Contract the CSHP as a separate pay item to promote the safety of the workers and the public; and (ii) regularly monitor the implementation of infrastructure projects for the timely identification of lapses in project implementation and the introduction of appropriate interventions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) maximize the use of LDDRMF in a manner consistent with regulations; (b) re-assign the two organic staff of DRRMO to its mother office; and (c) if the budget permits , fill in the remaining plantilla position of LDRRMO I, in compliance with Section 12 of RA No. 10121 and the NDRRMC/DILG/DBM JMC No. 2014-1.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed, through the Bids and Awards Committee to (a) instruct the BAC-TWG to strictly conduct post qualification review of bidder’s documents; (b) explain why the paid contract for ₱1,674,213.00 should not be disallowed in audit and the contract for ₱716,801.00 should not be terminated; (c) consider initiating blacklisting procedures against the erring supplier without prejudice to the filing of appropriate charges against the same, as warranted; (d) ensure completeness and chronology of dates of procurement documents; and (e) henceforth strictly adhere to the IRR of RA 9184 in the conduct of future procurements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the GFPS  to (a) continue maintaining the GAD database in compliance with JMC 2013-01; (b) facilitate and monitor the implementation of the agency’s GAD- related programs, activities and projects and ensure that these are effectively and efficiently implemented; and (c) seek acknowledgment from DILG on any changes or re-appropriation of future GPBs.

We commended the efforts of Management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the existing tax laws and regulations particularly on the withholding of taxes and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We commended the efforts of Management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on the withholding of GSIS contributions and loan amortization due from employees and remittance thereof including government share within the prescribed period.

We commended the efforts of management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on the withholding of PhilHealth contributions and loan amortization due from employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We commended the efforts of Management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continuously comply with the existing rules and regulations on the withholding of HDMF contributions and loan amortization due from employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.
We commended Management on its continued observance of the laws and regulations on the receipt and use of government funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MDRRMO to

(a) coordinate with barangay and other national government officials for the relocation of the temporary isolation facility of the LGU, with appropriate consideration of availability of funds; 

(b)ensure full compliance with the requirements set by the DOH and DILG in establishing an isolation facility; and 

(c) maintain the Municipality’s satisfactory performance in addressing the continuing COVID-19 crisis.
observations.managementAction:
Issued Executive Orders and Memorandum Order for COVID-19 for the implementation of protocols and guidelines of quarantine procedure at the barangay level. 

Facilitated necessary documents equipment, safety protocols, procedures and guidelines for the approval of DOH and Philhealth accreditation and certification of Municipal Quarantine Facility

(a) facilitate the annual registration with the LTO of all its motor vehicles, simultaneous with placing the same under GSIS insurance coverage; and

 (b) refrain from authorizing the use of said vehicles unless these are duly registered and properly insured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to enforce the mandatory Net Take Home Pay to all Municipal employees by limiting the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the GAA.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the 

(a) Municipal Budget Officer to observe the statutory budget requirement for the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; 

(b) BAC members and the Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities to pave way for their timely implementation and completion of the projects; and 

(c) Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) and the Municipal Engineer to regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time for optimum attainment of the intent of the 20% Development Fund; and

We recommended that the Municipality adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 325(a) of Republic Act No. 7160 in the preparation and approval of the annual budget of the LGU to avoid disallowance in audit. We also recommended that the Local Finance Committee and heads of departments closely coordinate as regard to budgeting and appropriation and properly exercise their functions in accordance with law.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Property and Supply Officer and the Municipal Assessor:

Retrieve the copies of deeds of sale or donation and proceed with the titling/registration of the land/lot being claimed by the Municipality; and

Make representation with proper agencies with respect to disposable public land in possession of the Municipality in order to register the land in its name.
observations.reason: Titling of the land is still ongoing

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Personnel Officer hire job order personnel based on necessity for a period not exceeding six months.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MDRRMO to (a) coordinate with barangay and other national government officials for the relocation of the temporary isolation facility of the LGU, with appropriate consideration of availability of funds; (b) ensure full compliance with the requirements set by the DOH and DILG in establishing an isolation facility; and (c) maintain the Municipality’s satisfactory performance in addressing the continuing COVID-19 crisis.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) General Services Officer to perform the duties and responsibilities outlined under Section 490(b) of the Local Government Code RA 7160; (b) Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (MDRRMO) to furnish the Accounting Office and Committee on Physical Inventory of PPE, a copy of Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM and Acknowledgement Receipts for In-kind Donations for DRRM as bases of recording in the Municipality’s books and reports; (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) record In-kind Donations received in accordance with Annex Q of COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2004; and (ii) disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements information pertaining to DRRM particularly the quantity received, issued and balance of donated relief goods as well as the receipt of donated PPE (if any) and its disposal/transfer; and (d) Committee on Physical Inventory of PPE to include the In-kind Donations received in the physical count and its corresponding Inventory Report.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Engineer to assign a project engineer for each project to ensure that the project will be completed on time and executed in accordance with the plan; and (b) Bids and Awards Committee to (i) consider the general policy of the government in acquiring brand new equipment after appropriate consideration of availability of funds; and (ii) benchmark from the provisions of DBM NBC 446-A when acquiring second-hand vehicles.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to (a) fully capacitate the LDRRM Office and comply with Section 12(b) of the Republic Act No. 10121 and all its legal requirements; and (b) if funds allow, consider creating positions at the MEO and re-assign the two DRRM staff to perform their mandated duties at the DRRM Office.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the designated General Service Officer to (a) facilitate the annual registration with the LTO of all its motor vehicles, simultaneous with placing the same under GSIS insurance coverage; and (b) refrain from authorizing the use of said vehicles unless these are duly registered and properly insured.
We recommended and the LCE concurred to instruct the GAD Focal Point System to (a) expedite the establishment of GAD Database; and (b) conduct GAD planning and budgeting based on GAD data base.

We commended the efforts of management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continue its compliance with the BIR regulations on withholding of taxes and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We commended the efforts of management and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to instruct the Accounting Office to continue its compliance with law and regulations on withholding of GSIS dues from employees and the timely remittance thereof.

We commended the efforts of management, especially the Accounting Office, and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to continue its compliance with the existing rules and regulations on withholding of Philhealth premium contributions of employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed period and recommended that they sustain their very satisfactory observance of related regulations.

We commended the efforts of management, especially the Accounting Office, and recommended, on which the LCE agreed, to continue its compliance with the existing rules and regulations on withholding of HDMF dues from employees and the timely remittance thereof.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) invest on its personnel in the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Office by providing them the necessary seminar/training pertinent to their function; (b) prioritize the procurement of disaster related equipment and; (c) comply with Rule 6 of RA No. 10121 for Local DRRM office minimum staffing requirement.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) cause the replacement of the vehicle notwithstanding the filing of appropriate action against the erring supplier; (b) ensure that the members of the Inspection Committee possess the requisite expertise to allow the proper discharge of the committee’s function; and (c) require the BAC and TWG to show the basis for the ABC of the dump truck, which we will evaluate.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to enforce the mandatory Net Take Home Pay to all Municipal employees by limiting the payroll deductions to the order of preference provided in the GAA.

11.11	We reiterated our prior year's recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
(a) encourage and direct the GFPS Committee and Technical Working Group to perform its mandated tasks;
(b) ensure that at least 5% of the total LGU's Annual Budget will be appropriated and correspond to the activities supporting GAD PPAs; and
(c) establish and maintain a GAD Database to ensure responsiveness of GAD Plan and Budget to gender issues, woman empowerment, and to sustain the LGUs critical consciousness in supporting gender and development.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Municipal Budget Officer to observe the statutory budget requirement for the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; (b) BAC members and the Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities to pave way for their timely implementation and completion of the projects; and (c) Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) and the Municipal Engineer to regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time for optimum attainment of the intent of the 20% Development Fund; and (d) Municipal Engineer to demand from the respective contractors for the repair of the defective and damaged portion of the road projects pursuant to the one year defects liability as stipulated in Section 62 of the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and management agreed that the (a) Disaster Risk Reduction Management (DRRM) Council (i) strictly adhere to the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 for the utilization of the LDRRMF of the municipality in order to further strengthen its disaster preparedness; (ii) Give priority to the procurement of basic rescue and response equipment and conduct a municipal-wide training over expenditures not related to DRRM; and (b) DRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit regularly the report on sources and utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the MENRO to conduct massive campaign on all barangays concerned to encourage residents/households towards the full implementation of the basic rules on waste segregation; (b) encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and ensure that the existing MRF of the barangays are fully functional; (c) recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of RA No. 9003; and (d) consider imposing fines and penalties for violators of the Anti-Littering policy as well as filing of administrative cases against erring government officials as provided by Sections  49  and 50  of RA No. 9003.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to develop, for approval of the Municipal Mayor and the Legislative Body, clear-cut, systematic and uniform policies and guidelines on the grant of financial assistance, which will cover, among others, the assessment, evaluation, grant and release of financial assistance pursuant to Section 36 of R.A. No. 7160; (b) Municipal Accountant to strengthen the internal control system over disbursements to ensure that a thorough review of the financial transactions prior to payment is made to have an assurance that expenditures are legal, proper and adequately documented. We also advice the Sangguniang Bayan through the Office of the Vice Mayor to (i) stop the granting of assistance as it is not within their mandated duties/functions as provided in Section 447 of RA 7160.

We recommended and management agreed the Local Chief Executive to (a) ensure that at least 5% of the total LGU budget appropriations under the Annual Budget correspond to the activities supporting GAD PPAs; (b) strive for the full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by utilizing the whole amount set aside for said purpose so that the desired outcomes are attained and benefits are enjoyed to the fullest by the intended recipients; (c) establish and maintain a GAD Database to ensure and sustain the LGUs critical consciousness in supporting gender and development, women’s empowerment and responding to gender issues; and (d) submit the reviewed and approved Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) by the DILG to COA Audit Team five (5) working days from date of approval.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned employee to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and duly accomplished Driver's Trip Tickets for all vehicles owned by the municipal government pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 in order to monitor vehicle utilization efficiently.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

 a) coordinate efforts and implement the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; 

b) involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws and intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents and tourists; and 


c) request the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the finalization of the ten-year SWMP for the early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003 and the prompt resolution thereof.

We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan pass ordinances for environment protection and preservation such as, mandatory segregation of wastes at source, “no segregation, no collection” policy;  “no littering” policy; and others.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive a) instruct the Inventory Committee to (i) conduct a comprehensive and complete physical count of PPE and (ii) submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; b) direct the General Service Office and the Municipal Accountant to complete/update the Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards and perform regular reconciliation thereof.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (a) determine the correct amount of RPT and SET collectibles from prior years; (b) Municipal Treasurer to henceforth furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year a certified list of taxpayer and the amount due and collectible for the year; and (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) recognize the RPT and SET Receivables in the books based on the said certified list and the list of delinquencies; and (ii) maintain subsidiary records for RPT and SET receivables.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to cause the provision of adequate budget for the bonding of all accountable officers of the LGU in amounts that are commensurate to the AOs’ accountabilities, in accordance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) instruct the (i) Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to indicate in the 20% Development Plan timelines for the implementation of development projects/programs/activities; (ii) BAC to align the timing of procurement processes with the timelines set in the 20% DF; (iii) heads of concerned offices to expedite the implementation and completion of development projects that were not yet started/completed as at December 31, 2017; (b) create a project monitoring committee that will (i) closely monitor the implementation of the development projects; and (ii) report to the Local Chief Executive any deviations noted; and (c) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 5 of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to assign LDRRMO Positions to the Office of the MDRRM and ensure that other personnel assign thereto has the competence/skills in disaster-related activities to ensure an effective and efficient delivery of disaster management-related services.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and the Solid Waste Management Board agreed to a) strictly impose the “no segregation, no collection” policy particularly at the Barangay level; and (b) facilitate the establishment of functional MRF.

We also advised the Sangguniang Bayan to pass ordinances on environment protection and preservation in accordance with provisions of R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and the Local School Board agreed to (a) prepare a comprehensive annual plan and budget for SEF with adequate consideration of the actual, realistic and more pressing needs of the public school sector; and (b) monitor the implementation of PPAs vis-à-vis the milestones and timelines set to ensure optimum use of the fund.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to (a) issue Certificate of Final Acceptance after one year from the date of Certificate of Completion or after the lapse of the defects liability period; (b) ensure that repair works are undertaken by the contractor on the defects/damages noted before the said certificate is issued; and (c) ensure that adequate stipulations for warranty on any liability or defects noted are provided in the contract.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure observance of the rules and regulations regarding the timely payment of representation and transportation allowances, and that in no time shall the allowances be given in advance or when services are not yet rendered. Accordingly, payments should be supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) comply with the minimum requirement on the allocation of the prescribed budget for GAD; and (b) instruct the GAD Focal Point/Person to provide details for the reported ₱6,478,305.65 GAD Accomplishment.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) GSO to (i) evaluate operational efficiency of the vehicles; (ii) determine whether disposal of these properties would be more cost-efficient than to maintain the same, with appropriate consideration of its impact on the flow or disruption of operation;  (iii) establish or prove the necessity and reasonableness of spare parts of motor vehicles that will be procured; and (iv) ensure that parts replaced for various motor vehicles are supported with Waste Materials Report; (b) instruct the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the Heads of Office to gradually impose savings for the funding of acquisition of new vehicles; and (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all the basic data or information are supplied in the Purchase Order.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continue being compliant with the revenue regulations on the withholding of taxes from the salaries and wages of personnel and payments to suppliers/contractors for the purchase of goods and infrastructure projects and the remittance thereof to the BIR within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continuously being compliant with the existing rules and regulations on withholding of GSIS dues from employees and remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continuously being compliant with the existing rules and regulations on withholding of HDMF contributions and repayments of loan amortization from salary of personnel and on remittance thereof to the HDMF within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

 instruct the BUB Focal Person of the respective implementing offices to strictly monitor the  implementation of projects and take note of any gaps/issues for future references or subsequent administrative action; and 
 
instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Accountant  to strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 for projects involving procurement and submit the lacking documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001, to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
 
direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee, to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and cause the submission of the required and necessary supporting documents of the subject disbursements; and 
 
instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required documents are submitted/attached to the DVs before their processing, in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and DRRM Officer to: (a) refrain from using DRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related with pre-disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations; (b) conduct seminars/trainings and other related programs within the province so LDRRMF could be maximized; and (c) strictly adhere with the guidelines on the utilization of the 5% DRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: Although the Municipal Accountant claimed that she ensured that complete documentary requirement were attached and disbursements made from LDRRMF were appropriate in nature and in accordance with the approved LDRRM plan, the same observation was noted during the audit.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No.  3 Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Budget Officer to (a) discontinue the charging of ineligible expenses to SEF to avoid disallowance; and (b) strictly comply with the provisions of RA Nos. 5447 and 7160 on the proper utilization of the fund to maximize the benefits therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The same observation was noted in post audit of transactions charged to SEF.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the report

observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, the Municipal Accountant complied on the documentary requirement for payment of payrolls of job order personnel. However, JOs are still hired for a period exceeding 6 months.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the MENRO  (a) exert effort to properly operate and maintain the Sanitary Landfill; (b) involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws and intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents and tourists; (c) seek the help of private companies/ organizations in the conduct of a more aggressive and intensive information campaign on the proper segregation of waste and “no littering” policies; and (d) exercise strong political will to implement the environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment as mandated under Republic Act  No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The LCE had designated a MENRO to supervise the SWM of the LGU.  Reiterated in Finding No. 4 Part II of this report.
observations.reason: No permanent personnel designated/ assigned to oversee the implementation of SWM

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) instruct the BUB Focal Person of the respective implementing offices to strictly monitor the  implementation of projects and take note of any gaps/issues for future references or subsequent administrative action; and  (b) instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Municipal Accountant  to strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 for projects involving procurement and submit the lacking documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001, to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee, to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and cause the submission of the required and necessary supporting documents of the subject disbursements; and  (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required documents are submitted/attached to the DVs before their processing, in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2016.

We recommended that the Local  Chief Executive direct:
the Disaster Risk Reduction Management (DRRM) Officer regularly monitor and evaluate the utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) based on the PPAs listed in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan (LDRRMP);
the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to  strictly adhere with the guidelines on the utilization of the 5% DRRM Fund; and
the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the end-users prepare the Requisition and Issue Slips for the issuances and utilization Drugs and Medicines Inventory and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies inventory to serve as basis for recording and reporting in the books.

We recommended that the Local School Board and the District Supervisor be guided on the proper utilization of SEF and ensure that only the authorized priority expenditures identified under the SEF mandate are included in the SEF Annual Budget.

We recommended that the management stop the practice of paying fixed allowances or fixed reimbursements for communication without the submission of proof on the incurrence of expense showing or justifying the actual usage or consumption by the concerned officials and employees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
refrain from requesting or accepting funds and pursuing an income-generating project in the absence of adequate study concerning issues in socio-economic, technical, marketing, management and operation; and
institute appropriate action to preserve the structure and equipment of the Iceplant by seeking technical assistance from proper agency or consider possible divestment scheme to recover residual value of the project investment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) coordinate efforts and implement the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; (b) involve closely the Barangay officials in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws and intensify the information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no-littering policy” among the residents and tourists; and (c) request the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the finalization of the ten-year SWMP for the early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003 and the prompt resolution thereof.
We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan pass ordinances for environment protection and preservation such as, mandatory segregation of wastes at source, “no segregation, no collection” policy;  “no littering” policy; and others.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) ensure that at least 5% of the total LGU budget appropriations under the Annual Budget shall correspond to the activities supporting GAD PPAs; (b) strive for the full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by utilizing the whole amount set aside for said purpose so that the desired outcomes are attained and benefits are enjoyed to the fullest by the intended beneficiaries; and (c) instruct the members of the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and TWG to ensure the reliable reporting of GAD accomplishments which are based on actual costs as substantiated by evidence appearing in the Accounting Office .

We recommended that the Municipality adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 325(a) of Republic Act No. 7160 in the preparation and approval of the annual budget of the LGU to avoid disallowance in audit.  We also recommended that the Local Finance Committee and heads of departments closely coordinate as regard to budgeting and appropriation and properly exercise their functions in accordance with law.

12.3 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) continue to faithfully comply with its duty to withhold taxes due on compensation and all money payments to suppliers, contractors and other creditors and promptly remit the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue in order to contribute to the government’ revenue-generation efforts; and (b) comply strictly with Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 and BIR Tax Revenue Regulation No. 10-2008.

We recommended and the management agreed to continuously comply with and adhere to the existing rules and regulations on withholding GSIS dues from employees, remitting the same including the mandatory employer’s share and performing the required reportorial functions.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Property and Supply Officer and the Municipal Assessor: 
retrieve the copies of deed of sale or donation and proceed with the titling/registration of ownership of the land/lot being claimed by the Municipality; or
Make representation with proper agencies with respect to disposable public land in possession of the Municipality in order to register the land in its name.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had forwarded its application for the 8 lots to the Regional DENR Office.  The application was then returned to the CENRO Casiguran tasked to work on special patent.
observations.reason: They were still waiting for the judicial form requested by CENRO from DENR Regional Office.  While for the two titled lots purchased, they still need funding for transfer taxes including penalties incurred.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to: 

prepare adjusting entries to correct the understatement and overstatement of the affected accounts;  and 

thereafter, ensure that the useful life of PPE prescribed PPSAS No. 17 is used to ensure the correctness of the amount of depreciation of assets.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary corrections as recommended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant, in coordination with other offices involve; (a) work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies; (b) submit to the Office of the Auditor completely the duly certified contract documents including bidding and other required supporting documents within  the time frame set by existing rules and regulations to ensure timely review and evaluation of contracts; and (c) henceforth, ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DVs before their processing for payment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with other offices involved was able to work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and submit to the Audit Team the complete supporting documents of the suspended transactions of CY 2015.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was able to submit advance copy of contract and its supporting documents within the required time, however, post audit of transactions still revealed deficient documentary requirements in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects.
